Calibration Workflow

Source: vignettes/calibration-workflow.Rmd
calibration-workflow.Rmd

This vignette describes the Poisson likelihood calibration workflow and the profile likelihood summaries used in the Hermosillo 2010 study.

library(twostraindengue)

study_data <- load_study_data(rows = 35:40)
all_parameters <- default_parameters()
fixed_names <- c(
  "LambdaM", "LambdaS", "Lambda1", "alphaC", "alphaH",
  "b", "muH", "muM", "vsigma", "vtheta", "p"
)

surface <- evaluate_loglikelihood_grid(
  x_values = seq(1.6, 2.0, length.out = 3),
  y_values = seq(0.004, 0.006, length.out = 3),
  evaluator = function(par) {
    negative_loglikelihood_original_scale(
      par = par,
      observation_times = study_data$week - min(study_data$week),
      observed_cases = as.matrix(study_data[, c("classical", "hemorrhagic")]),
      initial_state = default_state(all_parameters),
      time_grid = seq(0, 5, by = 0.1),
      fixed_parameters = all_parameters[fixed_names]
    )
  }
)

surface
#>          [,1]      [,2]      [,3]
#> [1,] 820.0012  908.3819  978.6167
#> [2,] 890.6113  973.8281 1032.2479
#> [3,] 949.5048 1022.6786 1062.1019
profile_interval(seq(1.6, 2.0, length.out = 3), relative_likelihood(apply(surface, 1, max)))
#> lower upper 
#>     2     2

This compact example mirrors the full calibration workflow while keeping the grid intentionally small for fast vignette builds.

Developed by Manuel Cota.
